data.examName: AP 宏观经济 2023年国际卷

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Which statement best describes the law of demand?

optionList.optionContent: When price increases, demand decreases.
When price increases, demand increases.
Price and quantity demanded are inversely related.
Price and quantity demanded are directly related.
The demand curve is always upward sloping.

questionOption:
A .

When price increases, demand decreases.

B .

When price increases, demand increases.

C .

Price and quantity demanded are inversely related.

D .

Price and quantity demanded are directly related.

E .

The demand curve is always upward sloping.

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: GDP is an imperfect measure of a nation’s economic performance because it does not account for which of the following?

optionList.optionContent: Purchases made by the nation’s government
Purchases of domestically produced goods by international consumers
Purchases of internationally produced goods by domestic consumers
Goods produced at home for personal consumption
Personal consumption expenditures on durable goods

questionOption:
A .

Purchases made by the nation’s government

B .

Purchases of domestically produced goods by international consumers

C .

Purchases of internationally produced goods by domestic consumers

D .

Goods produced at home for personal consumption

E .

Personal consumption expenditures on durable goods

questionList.questionTitle: Government spending effect on output and PL
questionList.choiceQuestionContent:
How will an increase in government spending with no crowding out affect real output and the price level in the short run?

Real OutputPrice Level
ADecreaseIncrease
BIncreaseDecrease
CIncreaseIncrease
DNo changeIncrease
ENo changeNo change

optionList.optionContent: A
B
C
D
E

questionOption:
A .

A

B .

B

C .

C

D .

D

E .

E

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: If a banking system has ample reserves, which of the following is an action taken by the central bank that would cause a decrease in the cyclical rate of unemployment in the short run?

optionList.optionContent: An increase in the required reserve ratio
An increase in the policy rate
An increase in the discount rate
A decrease in interest on reserves
A decrease in personal income tax rates

questionOption:
A .

An increase in the required reserve ratio

B .

An increase in the policy rate

C .

An increase in the discount rate

D .

A decrease in interest on reserves

E .

A decrease in personal income tax rates

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: When the government invests in education and physical capital, which of the following will happen in the long run?

optionList.optionContent: Real output per worker will decrease.
There will be upward movement along the aggregate production function.
There will be downward movement along the aggregate production function.
The aggregate production function will shift upward.
The aggregate production function will shift downward.

questionOption:
A .

Real output per worker will decrease.

B .

There will be upward movement along the aggregate production function.

C .

There will be downward movement along the aggregate production function.

D .

The aggregate production function will shift upward.

E .

The aggregate production function will shift downward.

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Which account includes both net exports and net unilateral transfers?

optionList.optionContent: The money market account
The trade balance account
The capital and financial account
The foreign investment account
The current account

questionOption:
A .

The money market account

B .

The trade balance account

C .

The capital and financial account

D .

The foreign investment account

E .

The current account

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Assume that a country has limited reserves in its banking system. Which of the following would lead to inflation in the long run according to the quantity theory of money?

optionList.optionContent: Sustained purchases of government bonds by the central bank
Commercial banks holding more reserves
Consumers spending more money as their confidence grows
An increase in the federal budget surplus
Business taxes decreasing and firms spending more money on capital improvements

questionOption:
A .

Sustained purchases of government bonds by the central bank

B .

Commercial banks holding more reserves

C .

Consumers spending more money as their confidence grows

D .

An increase in the federal budget surplus

E .

Business taxes decreasing and firms spending more money on capital improvements

questionList.questionTitle: France Italy olives grapes opportunity cost
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: If France has a comparative advantage in the production of grapes, and Italy has an absolute advantage in the production of olives, which of the following statements must be true?

optionList.optionContent: France and Italy will not benefit from trading with each other.
France can produce more olives than Italy can.
France can produce more grapes than Italy can.
France has a lower opportunity cost in producing grapes than Italy does.
France has a higher opportunity cost in producing grapes than Italy does.

questionOption:
A .

France and Italy will not benefit from trading with each other.

B .

France can produce more olives than Italy can.

C .

France can produce more grapes than Italy can.

D .

France has a lower opportunity cost in producing grapes than Italy does.

E .

France has a higher opportunity cost in producing grapes than Italy does.

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: If the number of people that are employed is 140 million, the number of people that are unemployed is 20 million, and the civilian, noninstitutional population aged 16 and over is 200 million, then the labor force participation rate is

optionList.optionContent: 10.0%
12.5%
60.0%
70.0%
80.0%

questionOption:
A .

10.0%

B .

12.5%

C .

60.0%

D .

70.0%

E .

80.0%

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Assuming the marginal propensity to consume is less than 1, which of the following describes why the magnitude of the tax multiplier is smaller than the magnitude of the expenditure multiplier?

optionList.optionContent: Changes in income taxes initially affect both household consumption and household savings.
Changes in income taxes initially affect household savings only.
Changes in income taxes initially affect net investment instead of consumption.
Changes in income taxes initially affect exports only.
Changes in income taxes initially affect firms only.

questionOption:
A .

Changes in income taxes initially affect both household consumption and household savings.

B .

Changes in income taxes initially affect household savings only.

C .

Changes in income taxes initially affect net investment instead of consumption.

D .

Changes in income taxes initially affect exports only.

E .

Changes in income taxes initially affect firms only.

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Which of the following best represents an investment in human capital?

optionList.optionContent: A clothing manufacturer purchases automated machinery.
An automobile manufacturer hires an engineer who specializes in maintaining modern robotic assembly machines.
A bank pays the tuition for one of its managers to pursue a degree in business administration.
A paper mill offers a bonus to an employee who develops a more efficient way to incentivize workers to work more night shifts.
A manufacturing firm builds a new assembly line.

questionOption:
A .

A clothing manufacturer purchases automated machinery.

B .

An automobile manufacturer hires an engineer who specializes in maintaining modern robotic assembly machines.

C .

A bank pays the tuition for one of its managers to pursue a degree in business administration.

D .

A paper mill offers a bonus to an employee who develops a more efficient way to incentivize workers to work more night shifts.

E .

A manufacturing firm builds a new assembly line.

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: If the inflation rate increases, what will happen to the real interest rate on existing loans with fixed nominal interest rates?

optionList.optionContent: It will increase.
It will decrease.
It will stay the same.
It will be greater than the nominal interest rate.
It will equal the unexpected inflation rate minus the expected inflation rate.

questionOption:
A .

It will increase.

B .

It will decrease.

C .

It will stay the same.

D .

It will be greater than the nominal interest rate.

E .

It will equal the unexpected inflation rate minus the expected inflation rate.

questionList.questionTitle: Interest rates and balance of payment
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Assume that a nation’s capital and financial account is in balance. If the nation’s central bank implements contractionary monetary policy, which of the following will occur solely as a result of the change in the nation’s interest rates?

optionList.optionContent: There will be an increase in financial capital outflows.
The balance of payments will be greater than zero.
Both the current account and the capital and financial account will move into deficit.
International investors will buy less of the nation’s financial assets.
The capital and financial account will move into surplus.

questionOption:
A .

There will be an increase in financial capital outflows.

B .

The balance of payments will be greater than zero.

C .

Both the current account and the capital and financial account will move into deficit.

D .

International investors will buy less of the nation’s financial assets.

E .

The capital and financial account will move into surplus.

questionList.questionTitle: Reason for increase in national debt
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Which of the following could have caused the national debt to change from $12 trillion to $15 trillion?

optionList.optionContent: A household debt increase of $3 trillion
A government budget deficit of $3 trillion
A government budget surplus of $3 trillion
A decrease of $3 trillion in financial capital inflows
An increase of $3 trillion in business debt

questionOption:
A .

A household debt increase of $3 trillion

B .

A government budget deficit of $3 trillion

C .

A government budget surplus of $3 trillion

D .

A decrease of $3 trillion in financial capital inflows

E .

An increase of $3 trillion in business debt

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: What is definitely true in the short run about the economy if real output begins to increase again after months of decline?

optionList.optionContent: The economy is falling into recession.
The economy is exiting a business cycle trough.
The price level is decreasing.
The unemployment rate is increasing.
The purchasing power of nominal wages is increasing.

questionOption:
A .

The economy is falling into recession.

B .

The economy is exiting a business cycle trough.

C .

The price level is decreasing.

D .

The unemployment rate is increasing.

E .

The purchasing power of nominal wages is increasing.

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Using the graph provided of the long-run Phillips curve (LRPC), which of the following could cause the change from LRPC1 to LRPC2?

optionList.optionContent: An increase in the price of natural resources
An increase in government spending
An increase in the central bank’s administered interest rates
A decrease in unemployment insurance benefits
A decrease in net exports

questionOption:
A .

An increase in the price of natural resources

B .

An increase in government spending

C .

An increase in the central bank’s administered interest rates

D .

A decrease in unemployment insurance benefits

E .

A decrease in net exports

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: What is the fundamental problem that economics attempts to address?

optionList.optionContent: Determining the appropriate level of government spending in an economy
How to maintain full employment output
How to fulfill unlimited wants with limited resources
What policies will stimulate the most economic growth
Whether or not to restrict international trade to achieve more favorable terms of trade

questionOption:
A .

Determining the appropriate level of government spending in an economy

B .

How to maintain full employment output

C .

How to fulfill unlimited wants with limited resources

D .

What policies will stimulate the most economic growth

E .

Whether or not to restrict international trade to achieve more favorable terms of trade

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Why is the unemployment rate an imperfect measure of joblessness?

optionList.optionContent: It includes workers who are on strike.
It counts discouraged workers.
It excludes the frictionally unemployed.
It does not include the number of underemployed workers.
It overestimates the number of individuals who are self-employed.

questionOption:
A .

It includes workers who are on strike.

B .

It counts discouraged workers.

C .

It excludes the frictionally unemployed.

D .

It does not include the number of underemployed workers.

E .

It overestimates the number of individuals who are self-employed.

questionList.questionTitle: Relationship between bonds and interest rates
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: What is the relationship between the price of previously issued bonds and the prevailing market interest rate?

optionList.optionContent: The price of previously issued bonds is directly related to the prevailing market interest rate.
The price of previously issued bonds is unrelated to the prevailing market interest rate.
The price of previously issued bonds is inversely related to the prevailing market interest rate.
The price of previously issued bonds is equal to the prevailing market interest rate.
The price of previously issued bonds is less than the prevailing market interest rate.

questionOption:
A .

The price of previously issued bonds is directly related to the prevailing market interest rate.

B .

The price of previously issued bonds is unrelated to the prevailing market interest rate.

C .

The price of previously issued bonds is inversely related to the prevailing market interest rate.

D .

The price of previously issued bonds is equal to the prevailing market interest rate.

E .

The price of previously issued bonds is less than the prevailing market interest rate.

questionList.questionTitle: Country X FP and MP combined
questionList.choiceQuestionContent:
The economy of Country X is experiencing demand-pull inflation as a result of an increase in consumer spending. If the government and central bank of Country X want to reduce inflation and the banking system of Country X has ample reserves, which of the following combinations of fiscal and monetary policy would be most effective to achieve their goal in the short run?

Fiscal Policy ActionMonetary Policy Action
ADecrease government spendingIncrease administered interest rates
BDecrease government spendingDecrease administered interest rates
CIncrease income taxesDecrease administered interest rates
DDecrease income taxesIncrease administered interest rates
EIncrease transfer paymentsIncrease administered interest rates

optionList.optionContent: A
B
C
D
E

questionOption:
A .

A

B .

B

C .

C

D .

D

E .

E

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Wayland and Hytop are countries that trade with each other and have flexible exchange rates. Which of the following groups will benefit if Wayland’s currency depreciates relative to Hytop’s currency in the short run?

optionList.optionContent: Exporters in Hytop and importers in Hytop
Exporters in Hytop and importers in Wayland
Exporters in Hytop and exporters in Wayland
Importers in Hytop and importers in Wayland
Importers in Hytop and exporters in Wayland

questionOption:
A .

Exporters in Hytop and importers in Hytop

B .

Exporters in Hytop and importers in Wayland

C .

Exporters in Hytop and exporters in Wayland

D .

Importers in Hytop and importers in Wayland

E .

Importers in Hytop and exporters in Wayland

questionList.questionTitle: What is included in monetary base
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: The monetary base includes which of the following?

optionList.optionContent: Mutual funds in retirement portfolios
Savings accounts
Currency in circulation
Credit cards
Outstanding mortgage balances

questionOption:
A .

Mutual funds in retirement portfolios

B .

Savings accounts

C .

Currency in circulation

D .

Credit cards

E .

Outstanding mortgage balances

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Assume an economy is in long-run equilibrium. If there is an increase in short-run aggregate supply, how will it be demonstrated in the Phillips curve model?

optionList.optionContent: A movement along the short-run Phillips curve to the left of long-run equilibrium
A movement along the short-run Phillips curve to the right of long-run equilibrium
A shift of the short-run Phillips curve to the left
A shift of the short-run Phillips curve to the right
A shift of the long-run Phillips curve to the right

questionOption:
A .

A movement along the short-run Phillips curve to the left of long-run equilibrium

B .

A movement along the short-run Phillips curve to the right of long-run equilibrium

C .

A shift of the short-run Phillips curve to the left

D .

A shift of the short-run Phillips curve to the right

E .

A shift of the long-run Phillips curve to the right

questionList.questionTitle: Similarity of CPI and GDP deflator
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: In what way is the GDP deflator similar to the consumer price index?

optionList.optionContent: Both can be used to adjust nominal values to real values.
Both use select market baskets of goods and services to measure changes in the price level.
Both measure only the change in prices of domestically produced goods and services.
Both measure the potential output of an economy.
Both increase as the unemployment rate increases.

questionOption:
A .

Both can be used to adjust nominal values to real values.

B .

Both use select market baskets of goods and services to measure changes in the price level.

C .

Both measure only the change in prices of domestically produced goods and services.

D .

Both measure the potential output of an economy.

E .

Both increase as the unemployment rate increases.

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: In the country of Zeta, which of the following will cause aggregate demand to increase?

optionList.optionContent: The price of oil, a key productive resource in Zeta, increases.
Zeta’s government increases regulations on businesses.
The aggregate price level in Zeta decreases.
Zeta’s government increases spending.
A large number of firms in Zeta adopt a new production technology.

questionOption:
A .

The price of oil, a key productive resource in Zeta, increases.

B .

Zeta’s government increases regulations on businesses.

C .

The aggregate price level in Zeta decreases.

D .

Zeta’s government increases spending.

E .

A large number of firms in Zeta adopt a new production technology.

questionList.questionTitle: Reason for increase in physical capital formation
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Which of the following will increase physical capital formation in an economy in the long run?

optionList.optionContent: A decrease in financial capital inflows
A decrease in frictional unemployment
An increase in unemployment benefits
An increase in interest rates
An increase in total savings

questionOption:
A .

A decrease in financial capital inflows

B .

A decrease in frictional unemployment

C .

An increase in unemployment benefits

D .

An increase in interest rates

E .

An increase in total savings

questionList.questionTitle: Use GDP deflator to find Real GDP
questionList.choiceQuestionContent:
Year Nominal Gross Domestic Product (GDP) GDP Deflator
2019 $4,000 100
2020 $5,000 125

Based on the data provided in the table, which of the following conclusions can be drawn for the period 2019 to 2020?

optionList.optionContent: Nominal GDP increased by 40%.
Nominal GDP increased by 20%.
Real GDP increased by 25%.
Real GDP did not change.
The inflation rate was 20%.

questionOption:
A .

Nominal GDP increased by 40%.

B .

Nominal GDP increased by 20%.

C .

Real GDP increased by 25%.

D .

Real GDP did not change.

E .

The inflation rate was 20%.

questionList.questionTitle: US and Eurozone current and capital accounts
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: The United States and the Eurozone are trading partners with flexible exchange rates, and the United States has a capital and financial account surplus. If Europeans reduce their demand for United States financial assets, which of the following will occur?

optionList.optionContent: The natural rate of unemployment in the United States will decrease.
United States imports from Europe will increase.
The United States current account will move toward surplus.
The United States capital and financial account surplus will increase.
United States exports to Europe will decrease.

questionOption:
A .

The natural rate of unemployment in the United States will decrease.

B .

United States imports from Europe will increase.

C .

The United States current account will move toward surplus.

D .

The United States capital and financial account surplus will increase.

E .

United States exports to Europe will decrease.

questionList.questionTitle: Economic growth on AS AD model
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: How is long-run economic growth demonstrated in the aggregate demand–aggregate supply model?

optionList.optionContent: A rightward shift in the short-run aggregate supply curve
A leftward shift in the long-run aggregate supply curve
A rightward shift in the long-run aggregate supply curve
A rightward shift in the aggregate demand curve
A leftward shift in the aggregate demand curve

questionOption:
A .

A rightward shift in the short-run aggregate supply curve

B .

A leftward shift in the long-run aggregate supply curve

C .

A rightward shift in the long-run aggregate supply curve

D .

A rightward shift in the aggregate demand curve

E .

A leftward shift in the aggregate demand curve

questionList.questionTitle: Limited reserves monetary policy incr price level
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: If an economy is currently experiencing a decrease in the price level and the banking system has limited reserves, which of the following monetary policy actions can be implemented to reverse the change in the price level in the short run?

optionList.optionContent: Increasing the required reserve ratio
Increasing the policy rate
Increasing government spending
Selling bonds on the open market
Buying bonds on the open market

questionOption:
A .

Increasing the required reserve ratio

B .

Increasing the policy rate

C .

Increasing government spending

D .

Selling bonds on the open market

E .

Buying bonds on the open market

questionList.questionTitle: Law of supply and producer behavior
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: According to the law of supply, which of the following is true as the price of a good or service decreases?

optionList.optionContent: Producers are willing and able to sell more so they increase their sales.
Producers are willing and able to sell less because their production costs increase.
Producers are willing and able to sell less because their profits decrease.
Consumers are willing and able to buy more because they will switch from more expensive goods.
Consumers are willing and able to buy more because they will be able to afford a greater quantity.

questionOption:
A .

Producers are willing and able to sell more so they increase their sales.

B .

Producers are willing and able to sell less because their production costs increase.

C .

Producers are willing and able to sell less because their profits decrease.

D .

Consumers are willing and able to buy more because they will switch from more expensive goods.

E .

Consumers are willing and able to buy more because they will be able to afford a greater quantity.

questionList.questionTitle: Interest rates and fixed rate loans
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: After a fixed-rate loan is repaid, the borrower’s financial position will have improved if

optionList.optionContent: the inflation rate was higher than expected
nominal interest rates increased
real interest rates increased
financial capital outflow increased
the money supply decreased

questionOption:
A .

the inflation rate was higher than expected

B .

nominal interest rates increased

C .

real interest rates increased

D .

financial capital outflow increased

E .

the money supply decreased

Change in the BudgetChange in the CFA

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Assume the economy is initially in long-run equilibrium. Which of the following describes how the short-run effect of an increase in personal income taxes would be depicted in the Phillips curve model provided?

optionList.optionContent: A movement along SRPC1 from point A to point B
A movement along SRPC2 from point C to point D
A movement along LRPC from point C to point E
A shift from SRPC2 to SRPC3
A shift from SRPC3 to SRPC2

questionOption:
A .

A movement along SRPC1 from point A to point B

B .

A movement along SRPC2 from point C to point D

C .

A movement along LRPC from point C to point E

D .

A shift from SRPC2 to SRPC3

E .

A shift from SRPC3 to SRPC2

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Which of the following explains why firms choose to produce more output in the short run as the price level increases?

optionList.optionContent: Flexible input prices
Improved technology
Decreased subsidies
Sticky nominal wages
Increased purchasing power

questionOption:
A .

Flexible input prices

B .

Improved technology

C .

Decreased subsidies

D .

Sticky nominal wages

E .

Increased purchasing power

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: What is the opportunity cost of keeping cash at home?

optionList.optionContent: The ability to make purchases quickly
The growth in the real value of the money that occurs during inflationary periods
The lost income from a zero-interest savings account at the bank
The lost income from not investing the money in an interest-bearing financial asset
The increased risk of losing the money in an economic downturn

questionOption:
A .

The ability to make purchases quickly

B .

The growth in the real value of the money that occurs during inflationary periods

C .

The lost income from a zero-interest savings account at the bank

D .

The lost income from not investing the money in an interest-bearing financial asset

E .

The increased risk of losing the money in an economic downturn

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Which of the following describes the impact of an automatic stabilizer during an economic contraction?

optionList.optionContent: As incomes rise, more individuals move up to higher income tax brackets, causing an automatic tax increase and reducing consumption.
As prices fall, workers accept lower wages, causing short-run aggregate supply to increase.
As unemployment falls, fewer individuals receive unemployment benefits, reducing consumption.
As unemployment rises, the government implements a tax decrease, stimulating consumption.
As unemployment rises, more individuals receive unemployment benefits, stimulating consumption.

questionOption:
A .

As incomes rise, more individuals move up to higher income tax brackets, causing an automatic tax increase and reducing consumption.

B .

As prices fall, workers accept lower wages, causing short-run aggregate supply to increase.

C .

As unemployment falls, fewer individuals receive unemployment benefits, reducing consumption.

D .

As unemployment rises, the government implements a tax decrease, stimulating consumption.

E .

As unemployment rises, more individuals receive unemployment benefits, stimulating consumption.

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Assume the base year is 2000. If the consumer price index in 2014 was 125, it can be concluded that a typical market basket of goods in 2014 cost

optionList.optionContent: 125 percent more than the same basket bought in the base year
125 percent more than the same basket bought in 2013
25 percent less than the same basket bought in the base year
25 percent more than the same basket bought in 2013
25 percent more than the same basket bought in the base year

questionOption:
A .

125 percent more than the same basket bought in the base year

B .

125 percent more than the same basket bought in 2013

C .

25 percent less than the same basket bought in the base year

D .

25 percent more than the same basket bought in 2013

E .

25 percent more than the same basket bought in the base year

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Which of the following transactions would be counted as investment spending in the calculation of a nation’s gross domestic product?

optionList.optionContent: Robert buys 100 shares of stock in an energy company.
Ming purchases a new delivery truck for her bakery business.
Latasha pays a mechanic to repair her personal car.
Sam earns interest on Canadian government bonds.
Taylor receives unemployment benefits when he loses his job.

questionOption:
A .

Robert buys 100 shares of stock in an energy company.

B .

Ming purchases a new delivery truck for her bakery business.

C .

Latasha pays a mechanic to repair her personal car.

D .

Sam earns interest on Canadian government bonds.

E .

Taylor receives unemployment benefits when he loses his job.

questionList.questionTitle: Country S borrowing effect on RIR and currency
questionList.choiceQuestionContent:
Assume that Country S has an open economy and a flexible exchange rate. How will an increase in borrowing in Country S affect its real interest rate, and what will be the resulting effect on the value of Country S’s currency in foreign exchange markets?

Real Interest RateCurrency Value
AIncreaseAppreciate
BIncreaseDepreciate
CNo changeNo change
DDecreaseAppreciate
EDecreaseDepreciate

questionList.questionTitle: Immediate and max change on M1
questionList.choiceQuestionContent:
Assume that a banking system has limited reserves and the required reserve ratio is 0.25. If an individual deposits $1,000 cash into a checking account, what is the immediate effect on the M1 measure of the money supply and the maximum possible change in the M1 measure of the money supply?

Immediate Effect on M1Maximum Possible Change in M1
ANo change$1,000 increase
BNo change$3,000 increase
CNo change$4,000 increase
D$1,000 increase$3,000 increase
E$1,000 increase$4,000 increase

B
C
D

A .

A

B .

B

C .

C

D .

D

E .

questionList.questionTitle: Reason for increase PL and decrease RGDP
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Which of the following would explain an increase in the price level and a decrease in real GDP in the short run?

optionList.optionContent: Workers successfully negotiated for higher wages.
Consumers decreased their spending.
The government decreased corporate taxes.
The government decreased business regulations.
Firms increased their investment spending.

questionOption:
A .

Workers successfully negotiated for higher wages.

B .

Consumers decreased their spending.

C .

The government decreased corporate taxes.

D .

The government decreased business regulations.

E .

Firms increased their investment spending.

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Peter runs a small business mowing lawns and cleaning pools. The production possibilities curve provided shows the combinations of lawns mowed and/or pools cleaned that Peter can complete in a day. Which of the following is true concerning Peter’s production possibilities curve?

optionList.optionContent: Peter can mow more than 6 lawns if he doesn’t clean any pools that day.
Peter’s opportunity cost of mowing 1 lawn is 12 of a pool cleaned.
Peter’s opportunity cost of cleaning 1 pool is 12 of a lawn mowed.
Peter experiences increasing opportunity costs in cleaning pools.
Peter experiences increasing opportunity costs in mowing lawns.

questionOption:
A .

Peter can mow more than 6 lawns if he doesn’t clean any pools that day.

B .

Peter’s opportunity cost of mowing 1 lawn is 12 of a pool cleaned.

C .

Peter’s opportunity cost of cleaning 1 pool is 12 of a lawn mowed.

D .

Peter experiences increasing opportunity costs in cleaning pools.

E .

Peter experiences increasing opportunity costs in mowing lawns.

questionList.questionTitle: Market for clinkers at specific price
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Based on the provided graph of the market for clinkers, which of the following statements is true if the current price in the market for clinkers is $20?

optionList.optionContent: There is a shortage of 2 clinkers.
There is a surplus of 2 clinkers.
The market is in equilibrium.
The demand for clinkers will decrease.
The supply of clinkers will increase.

questionOption:
A .

There is a shortage of 2 clinkers.

B .

There is a surplus of 2 clinkers.

C .

The market is in equilibrium.

D .

The demand for clinkers will decrease.

E .

The supply of clinkers will increase.

questionList.questionTitle: Policy to increase rate of economic growth
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Which of the following is the best example of a policy action that will increase the rate of long-run economic growth?

optionList.optionContent: Increasing unemployment benefits
Increasing investment in energy-efficient technologies
Increasing subsidies to consumers for spending on nondurable goods
Decreasing personal income tax rates
Decreasing spending on early childhood education

questionOption:
A .

Increasing unemployment benefits

B .

Increasing investment in energy-efficient technologies

C .

Increasing subsidies to consumers for spending on nondurable goods

D .

Decreasing personal income tax rates

E .

Decreasing spending on early childhood education

questionList.questionTitle: Stalia expansionary MP SR effect on nat unemp rate
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Suppose the country of Stalia is currently experiencing a recession, and in response, the central bank carries out expansionary monetary policy. As a result, what will happen to its natural rate of unemployment in the short run?

optionList.optionContent: It will increase as frictional unemployment increases.
It will increase as structural unemployment decreases.
It will decrease as cyclical unemployment increases.
It will decrease as structural and cyclical unemployment decrease.
It will remain the same as cyclical unemployment decreases.

questionOption:
A .

It will increase as frictional unemployment increases.

B .

It will increase as structural unemployment decreases.

C .

It will decrease as cyclical unemployment increases.

D .

It will decrease as structural and cyclical unemployment decrease.

E .

It will remain the same as cyclical unemployment decreases.

questionList.choiceQuestionContent:

Government BudgetInterest Rates

BMove toward deficitIndeterminate
CRemain in balanceDecrease
DMove toward surplusDecrease
EMove toward surplusIndeterminate

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Without any policy interventions, the country of Myto has automatically returned to long-run equilibrium after experiencing a recessionary gap. Which of the following conditions must have existed for this self-adjustment to long-run equilibrium to occur?

optionList.optionContent: Trade unions were strengthened, providing more income for their members.
Industrial investment increased, which increased stock prices.
Nominal wages decreased as workers accepted cuts in their earnings.
The central bank added to its portfolio by purchasing bonds on the open market.
Wages and prices were sticky at levels of output less than full employment.

questionOption:
A .

Trade unions were strengthened, providing more income for their members.

B .

Industrial investment increased, which increased stock prices.

C .

Nominal wages decreased as workers accepted cuts in their earnings.

D .

The central bank added to its portfolio by purchasing bonds on the open market.

E .

Wages and prices were sticky at levels of output less than full employment.

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: If the money supply is $40,000, consumer spending is $30,000, and nominal output is $50,000, what is the velocity of money according to the quantity theory of money?

0.75
0.8
1.25

0.6

0.75

D .

questionList.questionTitle: Expenditure and tax multipliers and AD
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Suppose the marginal propensity to consume is 0.8. If government spending increases by $15 million and personal income taxes increase by $20 million, what will be the maximum possible change in aggregate demand in the short run?

optionList.optionContent: A decrease of $5 million
An increase of $5 million
A decrease of $15 million
An increase of $15 million
A decrease of $20 million

questionOption:

A decrease of $5 million

B .

An increase of $5 million

C .

A decrease of $15 million

D .

An increase of $15 million

E .

A decrease of $20 million

questionList.questionTitle: Nation K cause of decreased nominal interest rates
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Assume the banking system in Nation K has limited reserves. Which of the following would most likely lead to a decrease in nominal interest rates in Nation K?

optionList.optionContent: The central bank in Nation K buys government securities.
The central bank in Nation K increases the required reserve ratio.
The government of Nation K decreases income taxes.
The government of Nation K increases unemployment benefits.
The government of Nation K increases spending.

questionOption:
A .

The central bank in Nation K buys government securities.

B .

The central bank in Nation K increases the required reserve ratio.

C .

The government of Nation K decreases income taxes.

D .

The government of Nation K increases unemployment benefits.

E .

The government of Nation K increases spending.

questionList.questionTitle: Cause for increase in supply of dollar
questionList.choiceQuestionContent: The currency of the United States is the United States dollar (USD), and the currency of Mexico is the Mexican peso (MXN). The United States and Mexico are trading partners with flexible exchange rate systems. The graph provided shows the market for the United States dollar. Which of the following events would cause the shift in the supply of dollars from SUSD1 to SUSD2 shown in the graph?

optionList.optionContent: Americans increase their demand for vacations in Mexico.
Mexicans increase their demand for vacations in the United States.
The United States increases its exports of electronic goods to Mexico.
Real income in Mexico increases.
The price level in the United States decreases.

questionOption:
A .

Americans increase their demand for vacations in Mexico.

B .

Mexicans increase their demand for vacations in the United States.

C .

The United States increases its exports of electronic goods to Mexico.

D .

Real income in Mexico increases.

E .

The price level in the United States decreases.

optionList.optionContent: Nominal gross domestic product per capita
Real gross domestic product per capita
Government spending per capita
Private-sector spending per capita
Bond purchases per capita

questionOption:

Nominal gross domestic product per capita

B .

Real gross domestic product per capita

Government spending per capita

D .

E .

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: If the expected inflation rate is 6%, the nominal interest rate is 2%, and the actual inflation rate is 4%, what is the actual real interest rate?

-2%
2%
4%
6%

questionOption:

-2%

2%

4%

questionList.choiceQuestionContent: Which of the following statements best describes the long-run aggregate supply curve?

optionList.optionContent: It is vertical because wages and input prices are fixed in the long run.
It is vertical because wages and input prices are fully flexible in the long run.
It is vertical because potential output is determined by a country’s central bank.
It is upward sloping because wages and input prices are fixed in the long run.
It is upward sloping because savings increase as interest rates rise.

questionOption:
A .

It is vertical because wages and input prices are fixed in the long run.

B .

It is vertical because wages and input prices are fully flexible in the long run.

C .

It is vertical because potential output is determined by a country’s central bank.

D .

It is upward sloping because wages and input prices are fixed in the long run.

E .

It is upward sloping because savings increase as interest rates rise.

subjectiveQuestionList.questionContent:
Include correctly labeled diagrams, if useful or required, in explaining your answers. A correctly labeled diagram must have all axes and curves clearly labeled and must show directional changes. If the question prompts you to “Calculate,” you must show how you arrived at your final answer.

 

The economy of Shaunland is in long-run equilibrium.

(a) Draw a correctly labeled graph of the aggregate demand, short-run aggregate supply, and long-run aggregate supply curves, and show each of the following.

  • (i) The current equilibrium real output and price level, labeled Y1 and PL1, respectively
  • (ii) The full-employment output, labeled YF

(b) Assume that the price of steel increases and Shaunland relies heavily on steel in domestic production. On your graph in part (a), show the short-run effect of the increase in steel prices on the equilibrium real output, labeled Y2, and the equilibrium price level, labeled PL2.

(c) As a result of the increase in the price of steel, is Shaunland experiencing cost-push inflation, demand-pull inflation, or deflation? Explain.

(d) Assume no policy action is taken to restore full employment. Based on your answer to part (c), explain how the economy will adjust in the long run.

(e) Assume instead of waiting for the long-run adjustment process, the central bank of Shaunland is considering implementing monetary policy to offset the effect of the increase in steel prices on the price level. The banking system in Shaunland has limited reserves. Identify the appropriate open-market operation that the central bank of Shaunland would implement.

(f) Draw a correctly labeled graph of the money market in Shaunland and show the effect of the open-market operation identified in part (e) on the equilibrium nominal interest rate.

(g) Based on the change in the nominal interest rate shown on your graph in part (f), what will happen to each of the following?

  • (i) Shaunland’s actual unemployment rate in the short run. Explain.

    (ii) Shaunland’s international financial capital flows

Respond to all parts of the question.

questionList.questionTitle: US Japan fiscal policy foreign exchange

subjectiveQuestionList.questionContent:
Include correctly labeled diagrams, if useful or required, in explaining your answers. A correctly labeled diagram must have all axes and curves clearly labeled and must show directional changes. If the question prompts you to “Calculate,” you must show how you arrived at your final answer.

 

The economy of the United States is currently operating below full employment.

(a) Assume the government wants to increase aggregate demand by $200 billion and the marginal propensity to consume is 0.8.

  • (i) Calculate the minimum change and state the direction of change in government spending required to increase aggregate demand by $200 billion. Show your work.
  • (ii) Suppose instead the government chooses to change income taxes in order to increase aggregate demand. Calculate the minimum change and state the direction of change in income taxes required to increase aggregate demand by $200 billion. Show your work.

(b) Assume the United States and Japan are trading partners with flexible exchange rates. Based solely on the change in the price level resulting from the increased aggregate demand in the United States, will the United States’ net exports to Japan increase, decrease, or remain unchanged? Explain.

(c) The currency of the United States is the dollar (USD) and the currency of Japan is the yen (JPY). Draw a correctly labeled graph of the foreign exchange market for the dollar and show the effect of the change in net exports identified in part (b) on the international value of the dollar.

Respond to all parts of the question.

Include correctly labeled diagrams, if useful or required, in explaining your answers. A correctly labeled diagram must have all axes and curves clearly labeled and must show directional changes. If the question prompts you to “Calculate,” you must show how you arrived at your final answer.

 

The table provided shows macroeconomic data for the economy of Liang Island in 2020. The GDP deflator was 150 in 2020.

Macroeconomic Variables Value (in millions)
Personal Consumption Expenditures $70
Government Spending $25
Gross Private Domestic Investment $10
Personal Income $90
Exports $20
Imports $5
Population 5

(a) Calculate the nominal gross domestic product (GDP) in 2020. Show your work.

(b) Based on your answer to part (a), calculate the real GDP in 2020. Show your work.

(c) Explain the conceptual difference between nominal GDP and real GDP.

(d) Assume real GDP in 2019 was $120 million and the population of Liang Island did not change from 2019 to 2020. Based on your answer to part (b), did the economy of Liang Island experience economic growth in per capita terms in 2020? Explain using numbers.

(e) What is one specific fiscal policy action the government of Liang Island can take to promote long-run economic growth? Explain.

Respond to all parts of the question.

template.templateName: 模考模板- AP宏观经济

partList.directionsText:
MACROECONOMICS
SECTION Ⅰ
Time - 1 hour and 10 minutes
60 Questions


Directions: Each of the questions or incomplete statements below is followed by five suggested answers or completions. Select the one that is best in each case and then fill in the corresponding circle on the answer sheet.

MACROECONOMICS
SECTION Ⅱ
Total Time - 1 hour
Reading Period - 10 minutes
Writing Period - 50 minutes
3 Questions


Directions: You are advised to spend the first 10 minutes reading all of the questions and planning your answers. You will then have 50 minutes to answer all three of the following questions. You may begin writing your responses before the reading period is over. lt is suggested that you spend approximately half your time on the first question and divide the remaining time equally between the next two questions. Include correctly labeled diagrams, if useful or required, in explaining your answers. A correctly labeled diagram must have all axes and curves clearly labeled and must show directional changes. lf the question prompts you to "Calculate," you must show how you arrived at your final answer. Use a pen with black or dark blue ink.

You may plan your answers in this orange booklet, but no credit will be given for anything written in this booklet. You will only earn credit for what you write in the separate Free Response booklet.
